
Graphics Engine Bible 2026
Volume II Supplement

DirectX 9 Deferred Rendering, Forward+, and Modernization Notes

=====================================================================
Purpose
=====================================================================

This supplement extends Chapters 2 and 3 with DirectX 9-specific
engineering guidance. While many modern rendering techniques were
popularized during the DX11/DX12 era, numerous concepts can be adapted
to DX9-class engines, injectors, and legacy game modernization projects.

=====================================================================
DX9 Hardware and API Constraints
=====================================================================

Key limitations:

- Shader Model 3.0 maximum feature set
- No compute shaders
- No UAVs
- No descriptor heaps
- No command queues
- Limited MRT count
- Limited texture formats
- Limited threading support

These constraints significantly influence renderer architecture.

=====================================================================
Deferred Rendering Under DirectX 9
=====================================================================

Typical pipeline:

Depth Prepass
↓
G-Buffer Generation
↓
Light Accumulation
↓
HDR Composition
↓
Post Processing
↓
Presentation

Unlike DX11+ implementations, DX9 renderers must carefully manage
memory bandwidth and render target usage.

=====================================================================
Recommended DX9 G-Buffer Layout
=====================================================================

RT0
    Albedo.rgb
    Specular.a

RT1
    Normal.xy
    Roughness.a

RT2
    Material Flags
    AO
    Custom Data

Depth
    D24S8

The goal is minimizing MRT bandwidth while preserving quality.

=====================================================================
Normal Compression Example
=====================================================================

HLSL Shader Model 3.0:

float3 DecodeNormal(float2 enc)
{
    float2 f = enc * 2.0f - 1.0f;

    float z = sqrt(1.0f - saturate(dot(f,f)));

    return normalize(float3(f.x, f.y, z));
}

Packing normals reduces memory pressure considerably.

=====================================================================
DX9 Light Volume Rendering
=====================================================================

A common DX9 deferred lighting solution:

Point Lights:
    Sphere Volumes

Spot Lights:
    Cone Volumes

Workflow:

Render Light Volume
↓
Stencil Test
↓
Accumulate Lighting

This approach reduces expensive fullscreen lighting calculations.

=====================================================================
Stencil Optimization
=====================================================================

Stencil culling was one of the most important DX9 optimization
techniques.

Workflow:

1. Render light volume depth bounds
2. Populate stencil buffer
3. Shade only affected pixels

Benefits:

- Reduced overdraw
- Reduced pixel shader cost
- Better scalability

=====================================================================
HDR Rendering in DX9
=====================================================================

Common formats:

A16B16G16R16F
R11G11B10-like alternatives unavailable
FP16 preferred

Typical HDR chain:

Scene
↓
HDR Buffer
↓
Bloom
↓
Tone Mapping
↓
Color Grading
↓
Present

=====================================================================
ENB-Style Injector Architecture
=====================================================================

Common hook locations:

IDirect3DDevice9::Present
IDirect3DDevice9::EndScene
IDirect3DDevice9::Reset

Workflow:

Capture Backbuffer
↓
Apply Effects
↓
Output Final Frame

Many post-processing frameworks use this model.

=====================================================================
Simulating Modern Features in DX9
=====================================================================

Modern Feature      DX9 Alternative

SSAO                Depth-based AO
SSR                 Limited screen reflections
TAA                 Temporal accumulation hacks
PBR                 Approximate Cook-Torrance
Clustered Lighting  CPU-generated light lists
Volumetrics         Raymarching approximations

=====================================================================
Forward+ Concepts in DX9
=====================================================================

True Forward+ relies heavily on compute shaders.

DX9 lacks compute support.

Possible workaround:

CPU Tile Generation
↓
Upload Light Lists
↓
Pixel Shader Evaluation

While slower than DX12 implementations, this can still improve
performance in moderate-light-count scenes.

=====================================================================
Legacy Engine Modernization
=====================================================================

Applicable targets:

- GTA San Andreas
- Drift City
- Prism3D titles
- Gamebryo DX9 projects
- Custom MMO engines

Modernization opportunities:

- HDR rendering
- Deferred lighting
- Better shadowing
- Improved post-processing
- Material upgrades
- Reflection systems

=====================================================================
Production Recommendations
=====================================================================

For DX9 projects:

- Minimize MRT usage
- Prefer FP16 HDR buffers
- Compress normals aggressively
- Use stencil light volumes
- Reduce state changes
- Batch draw calls
- Profile bandwidth continuously

Many legacy engines remain surprisingly capable when modern rendering
ideas are adapted carefully to DX9 constraints.

End of Supplement.
